goal: Fix dropped card taps on the daily board during the cold-load pre-data render window (BUG-021 tail)
done: Guarded setupBoard's slot onclick on cards[i] so taps on cardless (not-yet-dealt) slots are a clean no-op. js/main.js, one guard added.
symptom: On a cold/slow daily board load, tapping a card the instant the backs appear does nothing — selection doesn't stick (counter stays 0/1); a tap after full load works.
root_cause: init() renders the daily board twice — once via initShell before loadTarotManifest resolves (state.cards empty -> getDrawableCards returns [] -> all 12 slots is-hidden/cardless), then again in the manifest .then() with data, which builds a fresh setupBoard and wipes the DOM (ensureSlots: boardEl.textContent=''). A tap in the first window toggled is-selected on a cardless slot (cards[i] undefined, onSelectionChange got []) and was then discarded by the rebuild.
fix: Added `if (!cards[i]) return;` at the top of the slot onclick in setupBoard, so an early tap on a not-yet-dealt slot is ignored until the card exists.
verified: chrome-devtools-mcp localhost, Slow 3G, iPhone 390x844: early tap on render-1 slot -> 'card-slot is-hidden' (no is-selected, guarded)
affects: daily.html selection board (and any setupBoard consumer: full/question boards — guard is universally correct)
notes: Mirror sync (www/ios) deferred — working tree carries pre-existing mirror drift (BUG-015); web serves canonical so the fix is live on push. iOS mirror needs separate reconciliation. BUG-021 also has a still-open follow-up: the 4.6MB cards.json payload cut.
